items.rationale: Seen as the first clear product-market fit in crypto and the 'killer app'; cheapest way to send a dollar, growth uncorrelated to crypto cycles and interest rates, with a regulatory tailwind (GENIUS/STABLE bills). Framed as money's 'WhatsApp moment' and the on-ramp to bring the next billion onchain.

citations.quote: Stablecoins are the "killer app" of crypto, and here's a bunch of great data that shows it.
Stablecoins have found product market fit and this chart shows it.
Stablecoins are our first real shot at doing for money what email did for communication: make it open, instant, and borderless.

items.rationale: Viewed as the native antidote to physical-infrastructure monopolies and the most salient path to making 'atoms look like bits.' Bet on user-owned/operated physical services; backed Daylight (energy). Repeatedly named the future of consumer crypto.

citations.quote: The future of consumer crypto? DePIN
DePIN provides the most salient path towards making atoms look more like bits for builders and investors alike.

items.rationale: Blockchains seen as essential to enforce ownership/identity/authenticity amid AI-generated content, to decentralize AI away from Big Tech, and to give autonomous agents a permissionless payment rail. Backed Story Protocol (IP layer).

citations.quote: AI needs blockchain-enabled computing.
AI agents can't have bank accounts, but they can have crypto wallets.

items.rationale: Held as the endgame for blockchain scalability and a new developer primitive that 'democratizes' SNARKs (Jolt). Long-term conviction high, but explicitly tempered in 2025 by security/performance immaturity.
items.age_note: Strong hype 2024 ('Fastest zkVM in the world'); deliberately cooled in 2025 to 'not ready for prime time' on security/perf grounds.

citations.quote: Today we're releasing Jolt Alpha ⚡️ ✅ Fastest zkVM in the world.
I still think ZK is under hyped. Let's talk about why

items.rationale: Treated as the next key bottleneck now that infra and adoption are maturing; privacy 'is essential.' Backed Aleo, Seismic (private shared state), and Miden (edge/client-side proving).
items.age_note: Emerging as a top priority in 2025; less emphasized earlier.

citations.quote: Disagree on Miden. Privacy is essential
This all sets up privacy as a key bottleneck and important product problem that wasn't the case years ago.

items.rationale: Considered to have durable usage that keeps growing; focus on lowering UX friction to convert passive holders into users (Legend) and on more expressive protocols via ZK coprocessors.

citations.quote: DeFi remains popular — and it's growing
leading the $15M seed round in @legendapp to simplify DeFi and help bring the next 100M users onchain.

items.rationale: Restaking framed as a marketplace for raw cryptoeconomic security that could unlock '100x' innovation on Ethereum; still cited in 2024 H2 as the security layer for real-time-proof rollups (t1).
items.age_note: Peak enthusiasm at Feb 2024 Series B; still load-bearing in late-2024 investments but less foregrounded by 2025.

citations.quote: EigenLayer has the potential to unlock 100x faster innovation on technologies like consensus mechanisms, novel virtual machines, decentralized oracles, bridges

items.rationale: Excited by interoperable, user-owned social graphs as a counter to closed platforms; vibrant dev ecosystems forming around open protocols.

citations.quote: The Decentralized Social experiment is underway, and vibrant developer ecosystems are emerging around open protocols like Farcaster.

items.rationale: Sees fragmentation as Ethereum's key rollup-era problem and seamless cross-rollup composability ('holy grail') as the fix; backed Espresso.

citations.quote: Seamless interoperability is my holy grail for rollups

items.rationale: The most sustained concern in the corpus: speculation with no inherent value deters quality founders, hands ammunition to politicized regulators, and damages adoption and the industry's long-term legitimacy. Distinguished sharply from value-creating network tokens.
items.age_note: Consistent stance 2023→2025; intensified around 2024 memecoin mania and the 2025 TRUMP/celebrity-coin wave.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the crypto equivalent of chain emails.
we shouldn't pretend that the casino doesn't set us back. Deterring great founders and giving ammo to politicized regulators has a profound effect.
Memecoins are the opposite of equity — ownership of nothing versus something.

items.rationale: Worried that dropping decentralization standards lets centrally-controlled, equity-like tokens (e.g., FTT-type) evade securities laws — a 'form over substance' loophole that would harm consumers and US capital markets. A 2025 flagship concern as the new meta of launching company tokens emerged.
items.age_note: Sharpened in early 2025 amid the post-election 'everything is legal' narrative.

citations.quote: Company-backed tokens are *not* network tokens.
this approach would incentivize the propagation of synthetic securities—issuers promising economic benefits that replicate securities, but evading securities laws

items.rationale: Easy early liquidity for insiders is seen as an existential threat that breeds get-rich-quick schemes and lets VCs/founders dump on retail (the SPAC analogy). Pushes 4yr/1yr cliffs and lockups tied to decentralization milestones; disclosure alone deemed insufficient.

citations.quote: Unfettered access to secondary market liquidity is an existential threat to innovation.
4 year/1 year cliff token lockups should be standard.

items.rationale: Explicit warning that treating trusted execution environments as equivalent to trustless consensus will 'lead to a lot of pain'; a security-architecture concern surfacing in 2025.

citations.quote: "Trusted" and "Trustless" are fundamentally different. The conflation of trusted execution environments with trustless blockchains is going to lead to a lot of pain.

items.rationale: Despite excitement, the team flags that production SNARKs are 'riddled with bugs,' protected mainly by obscurity, with formal verification years away — the biggest threat to ZK fulfilling its potential.

citations.quote: they need to be secure they're not now they're riddled with bugs
we flirt with disaster if we pretend they're ready for prime time.

items.rationale: Years of 'regulation-by-enforcement,' weaponized Howey, debanking and the IRS broker rule were treated as the dominant industry threat — but this concern has largely reversed into optimism in 2025 after dismissed cases, a new administration, and repealed rules.
items.age_note: Acute, top concern 2023–2024 under the Biden SEC; markedly eased in 2025 — now framed as 'at an end' with clarity expected this year. Residual worry shifts to getting market-structure legislation right and to plaintiffs' firms.

citations.quote: The weaponization of regulatory uncertainty by the SEC is at an end.
I'm more optimistic than I've ever been that crypto is going to get regulatory clarity this year.

items.rationale: Sees consultants manufacturing urgency to push US founders offshore; flags operational complexity, weaker geopolitical protection, tax risk, and value-extraction/conflicts. Argues the US is now a better place to build.
items.age_note: Crystallized late 2024 into 2025 alongside US regulatory optimism.

citations.quote: There couldn't be a more glaringly obvious sign that we need to kill foreign foundation structures.
some consultants are manufacturing a sense of urgency to push them into foreign foundations as early as possible... Be careful.

items.rationale: Research-grounded worry that proposer-builder separation decentralizes validation but centralizes block building via a 'proof-of-MEV' feedback loop (worsened by multi-block/cross-chain MEV).

citations.quote: PBS is reducing centralization in validation, but might create centralization in building because of this disproof of MEV competition

items.rationale: Held up as the canonical decentralized network token: not a security, value accrues through protocol fees and a burn mechanism, expected to be deflationary long-term. Repeatedly the reference example of a 'good' token.

citations.quote: ETH is not a security, nor should transactions in ETH be securities transactions.
long term I think most of us expect eth to be a deflationary currency

items.rationale: Treated as digital gold and the native asset of the most secure, decentralized network — distinct from memecoins despite 'memetic' qualities. Endorsed as a legitimate store of value.

citations.quote: digital gold—a store of value, albeit a volatile one.
being the native asset of what is arguably the most secure and decentralized network gives it economic characteristics... unlike any memecoin.

items.rationale: Conviction expressed via capital: an additional $55M token purchase with a 3-year lockup in April 2025, citing the protocol enabling new businesses and complex workflows onchain. Earlier praised LayerZero v2 for decoupling security from liveness.

citations.quote: we've invested an additional $55M in @LayerZero_Core tokens (ZRO), with a 3-year lockup.

items.rationale: Cited as a 'clear network token' whose network effects accrue to holders and which can capture protocol fees (UniStaker), contrasted favorably against company-backed tokens like FTT.

citations.quote: UNI is a clear network token and we discuss the example very clearly.
UNI can capture fees from the network (the switch is off right now) via a mechanism like UniStaker.

items.rationale: Recurs in the team's metrics as the leading chain by monthly active addresses and a driver of low-fee DEX volume; grouped with ETH/BTC as a bona-fide network token rather than a security. Endorsement is data-led rather than thesis-led.

citations.quote: The top chain by monthly active addresses: @solana.

items.rationale: Used as the archetype of value-less, purely speculative tokens that 'redistribute' rather than create capital and expose retail to harm; explicitly classified as ownership of nothing.

citations.quote: Memecoins are the opposite of equity — ownership of nothing versus something.
True memecoins have no intrinsic value (no utility, no company, no anything) and their price is driven purely by speculation

items.rationale: The recurring cautionary example of a 'synthetic security' — value derived solely from a centralized company (FTX), the same risk profile as equity, and what control-based frameworks must screen out. Cited as defunct/negative archetype, not an active position.

citations.quote: this approach would treat ETH (whose value is solely derived from a decentralized network) the same as FTT (whose value was solely derived from a centralized company, FTX).
secondary transactions of FTT should be subject to securities laws as the instrument was essentially a profits interest in FTX

risk_regime.rationale: As of April 2025 the house is markedly bullish and exhorting builders forward: regulatory wins (dismissed SEC cases, repealed IRS broker rule), burgeoning institutional adoption, all-time-high stablecoin volume exceeding Visa/Mastercard, dramatically cheaper infrastructure, and a clear agent-economy thesis. The optimism is structural and aimed at fundamentals/building, not speculation — explicitly paired with disdain for the memecoin 'casino' and insistence on decentralization, long lockups, and value-creating network tokens. Posture is 'caution good, cynicism self-defeating; keep building.'

notes: Corpus is dominated by a16z crypto voices spanning Oct 2023–Apr 2025 (notably Chris Dixon on networks/AI, Miles Jennings on token classification & regulation, Eddy Lazzarin and a research-led writer on infra/DePIN/ZK, Justin Thaler on SNARKs/Jolt, Scott Kominers on market design/IP, Tim Roughgarden-adjacent research on MEV/PBS). Heaviest single reversal: the regulatory-lawfare concern was the industry's top fear in 2023–2024 but flipped to optimism in 2025 after dismissed cases and a new administration — reported here at its current, lowered intensity. The token-classification framework (network vs. company-backed vs. memecoin vs. arcade/collectible, decentralization = absence of control) is the connective tissue between the team's excitement (network tokens, stablecoins) and concern (memecoins, synthetic securities). ZK/zkVMs appear in BOTH excited and concerned lists — high structural conviction on potential, high concern on near-term security/performance readiness — which is the team's genuine, internally consistent nuance rather than a contradiction. Conviction scores reflect stated intensity/frequency, not probability of payoff.
